description: Healthy-ish metric targets inherited from EFT original data. Treat as drift alarms, not laws. A match outside these ranges warrants investigation, not automatic rejection.
source: README.md Part V — Canonical Events And Telemetry

guardrails.name: average_possession_duration_sec
guardrails.description: Mean duration a player holds the ball before losing it (fumble, tackle, throw, goal).
guardrails.drift_alarm: If average exceeds 3s, possession is becoming sticky. If under 1s, possession is too chaotic to be readable.

guardrails.name: tackle_to_possession_ratio
guardrails.description: Ratio of TackleResolve events to PossessionTransfer events per match.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Below 2x: not enough physical contest — game is becoming a passing sim. Above 4x: too many fruitless tackles, possibly cooldown or hit-detection issues.

guardrails.name: goals_per_match
guardrails.description: Total goals scored per match. Map-dependent.
guardrails.notes: About 6 is the inherited reference. Adjust per-map — Slam Dunk may score higher due to multiple route pressure.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Under 4: scoring is too hard — map or physics may be breaking the sport. Over 10: scoring is too easy — defender viability is eroded.

guardrails.name: throw_catch_rate
guardrails.description: Fraction of ThrowAttempts (release phase) that result in a PossessionTransfer via catch.
guardrails.notes: About 35% in inherited mixed data. Lower = throws are risky/punished. Higher = passing is too safe.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Above 0.50: throwing is too safe — removes tackle pressure. Below 0.25: throwing is useless — removes passing as a viable option.

guardrails.name: head_on_log_rate
guardrails.description: Fraction of TackleResolve events classified as HeadOn (matched or near-matched frontal).
guardrails.notes: True frontal contests are a subset of all tackle events. Logged HeadOn events are the matched/near-matched subset only.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Below 0.10: head-on skill is underused — map geometry or player behavior avoiding frontal contact. Above 0.40: too many matched frontal collisions, possibly speed normalization issue.

guardrails.name: respawn_to_contest_sec
guardrails.description: Mean time from PlayerDeath to PlayerRejoinsContest.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Above 10s: players are irrelevant after dying — violates C-010. Below 2s: respawn is too close to the contest, removing death as a meaningful event.

guardrails.name: scrum_duration_sec
guardrails.description: Mean duration of ScrumDetected events before outcome (breakout, reset, or stalemate).
guardrails.notes: Often 2-5s in inherited data before breakout or reset.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Above 5s: scrums are stagnating — possession lock or physics issue. Below 1.5s: scrums are resolving too fast, removing tension.

guardrails.name: ball_loose_duration_sec
guardrails.description: Mean time the ball is loose (no holder) before PossessionTransfer or BallReset.
guardrails.drift_alarm: Above 4s: ball is consistently uncatchable — physics or auto-pickup issue. Below 0.5s: ball is almost never truly loose — reducing contest windows.
